name: From Multi-Agent to Single-Agent: When Is Skill Distillation Beneficial?
summary: arXiv:2604.01608v4 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Multi-agent systems (MAS) tackle complex tasks by distributing expertise, though this often comes at the cost of heavy coordination overhead, con...
description: arXiv:2604.01608v4 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Multi-agent systems (MAS) tackle complex tasks by distributing expertise, though this often comes at the cost of heavy coordination overhead, context fragmentation, and brittle phase ordering. Distilling a MAS into a single-agent skill can bypass these costs, but this conversion lacks a principled answer for when and what to distill. Instead,...
author: Binyan Xu, Dong Fang, Haitao Li, Kehuan Zhang
